title: 06 sky birds exchange complete world pods
prompt: REFERENCE AUTHORITY — Images 1 and 2 lock Liz and John, Peelerz bags, campus entrance, and identity. Image 3 supplies appetizing fruit-world density only. No current campus world-swap composition is inherited. ONE RULE — Colossal elegant birds exchange sealed translucent world-pods above the campus. When one pod descends, the existing world disappears in a strict sequence—extras, cars, signs, buildings— while the complete replacement world emerges from inside the same pod. FRAME — One cinematic continuous daylight wide. Liz and John remain on the central campus steps, eyes locked. Directly above them a crane-like sky bird releases a house-sized pearly grape pod. Across the left third, ordinary campus still exists but all background people have vanished; the central third has lost cars, signs, and buildings, exposing clean ground; across the right third, an intricate night ocean-city is physically unfolding from the descending pod, with luminous canals, boats, libraries, and fruit constellations. Three other enormous birds cross the upper sky carrying unopened mango, lychee, and mixed-flavor pods containing visibly different climates. VISUAL PROOF — single descending pod; ordered disappearance; replacement world emerging; other world-pods in transit. AVOID — portal rings, flying field islands, giant fruit face, destructive birds, eggs hatching animals, collage, empty abstract void, direct religious symbolism.

resolved_params.system_prompt: Create one extraordinary coherent cinematic Peelerz commercial styleframe, never a collage, contact sheet, storyboard grid, or design sheet. Treat the prompt as one continuous physical world photographed from one camera position. Favor the new whole-world rule over any existing car-peel, parachute, mecha, helper-crew, floating-field-island, sleeping-fruit-face, or paper-camera concept. Preserve only declared talent/location authorities. Make the impossible rule readable at a glance through large-scale spatial organization and specific ordinary details continuing inside it. Youthful, surprising, benign, appetizing, and not preschool-coded. No captions, labels, borders, watermarks, invented package copy, gore, weapons, portals, rainbow arches, generic magical particles, or gummy bears.
